audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra priestly rupture text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Nadab and Abihu's strange fire, priestly grief restrictions, alcohol/service warning, teaching discernment, remaining offering dispute, and Moses accepting Aaron's grief logic.

verses.restored: Nadab and Abihu, sons of Aaron, each took his firepan, put fire in them, placed incense upon it, and brought near before YHWH strange/unauthorized fire, which he had not commanded them.
Fire came out from before YHWH and consumed them, and they died before YHWH.
Moses said to Aaron, This is what YHWH spoke, saying, By those near me I will be sanctified, and before all the people I will be honored. Aaron was silent.
Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, sons of Uzziel uncle of Aaron, and said to them, Come near, carry your brothers from before holy place to outside camp.
They came near and carried them in their tunics to outside camp, as Moses spoke.

Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar his sons, Do not loosen hair of your heads and do not tear your garments, lest you die and wrath come upon all congregation; but your brothers, all house of Israel, shall weep for the burning that YHWH burned.
From entrance of tent of meeting you shall not go out, lest you die, for anointing oil of YHWH is upon you. They did according to word of Moses.
YHWH spoke to Aaron, saying,
Wine and strong drink you shall not drink, you and your sons with you, when you come into tent of meeting, so you do not die; age-long statute throughout your generations,
and to distinguish between holy and common, between unclean and clean,
and to teach children of Israel all statutes that YHWH spoke to them by hand of Moses.
Moses spoke to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar, his remaining sons, Take grain offering remaining from fire-offerings of YHWH and eat it unleavened beside altar, for Holy of Holies it is.
You shall eat it in holy place, for it is your due and your sons' due from fire-offerings of YHWH, for so I was commanded.

Breast of wave offering and thigh of contribution you shall eat in clean place, you and your sons and daughters with you; for as your due and your sons' due they are given from sacrifices of peace/well-being offerings of children of Israel.

Thigh of contribution and breast of wave offering, upon fire-offerings of fat portions, they shall bring to wave as wave offering before YHWH; it shall be for you and your sons with you as age-long due, as YHWH commanded.
Moses diligently sought goat of sin/misalignment offering, and look, it had been burned. He was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's remaining sons, saying,
Why did you not eat sin/misalignment offering in holy place? For Holy of Holies it is, and he gave it to you to carry iniquity of congregation, to make atonement/covering for them before YHWH.
Look, its blood/life was not brought into holy place inside; eating, you should have eaten it in holy place, as I commanded.

Aaron spoke to Moses, Look, today they brought near their sin/misalignment offering and burnt offering before YHWH, and such things happened to me. If I had eaten sin/misalignment offering today, would it have been good in eyes of YHWH?
Moses heard, and it was good in his eyes.

translation_notes: Strange/unauthorized fire is defined only as what YHWH had not commanded; avoid speculative certainty beyond text.
Aaron's silence should be preserved as grief/shock, not consent or lack of feeling.
The house of Israel is permitted to weep; priestly restriction is role-specific and ethically difficult.
Wine/strong drink prohibition follows rupture and relates to priestly discernment/teaching, but do not overclaim causality unless audited.
Moses accepts Aaron's refusal to eat after tragedy, showing grief can interrupt ritual expectation.
safety_note: Do not use Nadab and Abihu to terrorize people with arbitrary Divine violence or to suppress grief. The chapter is a severe priestly-boundary narrative; it also preserves communal mourning and Moses accepting Aaron's grief-shaped discernment.
